user_query:
I run a weekly highlights roundup for our lab focusing on achievements by women in primatology. Please set up a small, locally runnable weekly job that I can schedule, and run it once for the week ending Sunday, 2025-03-09.

Requirements:
1) Implement a command-line script under scripts/ (name your choice, e.g., scripts/generate_digest) that:
   - Reads input/achievements.csv.
   - Accepts an --as-of YYYY-MM-DD argument. Treat the "week" as the Monday–Sunday period that ends the day before the as-of date. For this run, use --as-of 2025-03-10, so the week is 2025-03-03 to 2025-03-09 (inclusive).
   - Filters rows to that week and sorts them by Date ascending.
   - Produces all three deliverables under output/ using the exact filenames below:
     a) output/weekly_digest_2025-03-10.md
        - Include a heading with the date range (2025-03-03 to 2025-03-09).
        - Include a table with columns: Date, Scientist, Category, Title (truncate to <=80 characters without breaking words), Affiliation.
        - Include a "Counts by Category" section listing each Category from the filtered items and its count.
     b) output/draft_email_2025-03-10.txt
        - First lines:
          Subject: Weekly highlights: female primatologists (2025-03-03 to 2025-03-09) — N items
          To: a comma+space-separated list of all emails from input/group_list.csv
        - Body: a short 2–3 sentence intro and a bullet list where each bullet matches the filtered items exactly (Scientist — Title (Date)). The bullet list should correspond one-to-one with the items in the digest table.
     c) output/meeting_notes_2025-03-10.md
        - Sections with these headings in order: "Highlights this week", "Discussion prompts", "Action items (owners, due date)".
        - "Highlights this week": list the same items as the digest table (one line per item: Date — Scientist — Category — Title).
        - "Discussion prompts":
          • For each Publication item, add: "Discuss methodology in [Title]."
          • For each Award item, add: "Consider nominating colleagues for similar awards."
          • For each Talk item, add: "Invite [Scientist] to present to our group."
        - "Action items (owners, due date)": at least 3 concrete follow-ups referencing specific items from this week. Assign owners using the first three names in input/group_list.csv in order (one action per person). Use a due date of 2025-03-14 for each.
   - Create output/run.log capturing: the exact command you ran, the resolved date range, the number of items selected, and the absolute path(s) of the files written.

2) Provide a thin wrapper script scripts/run_weekly.sh that invokes your generator with the correct --as-of date argument. For this submission, run it once with --as-of 2025-03-10 so the output files above are created.

3) Add a scheduling stub at output/schedule_example.txt containing a single cron line that would run scripts/run_weekly.sh every Monday at 08:00 local time (do not actually install it—just write the example line into the file).

Verification notes:
- Only items dated 2025-03-03 through 2025-03-09 (inclusive) should appear in all three deliverables.
- The digest table, the email bullet list, and the "Highlights this week" list must reference the same set of items in the same date order.
- The "Counts by Category" must match the categories present in that filtered set.
- The email's To header must include all emails from input/group_list.csv, joined by ", ".

Input files are in input/. Please produce all artifacts under output/ and scripts/ as named above. Run the job once so the outputs exist in this workspace. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
